memory.summary: I'm free but terrified I'll miss the deadline again. Let's just grab a quick win now, then head to the group for lunch.
I just got a win, but the project is still stuck at the start while my energy drops. This feels like a false victory that won't save me.
I finally got the quick win done, but my energy is tanking. I need to call the professor immediately; they're going to be furious if I miss the deadline.
I told the group to stop joking about the chore, but now I feel guilty for snapping. They'll think I'm unapproachable.
I just waited for the group, but now I'm worried they'll think I'm ignoring them. My energy is low, so I need to check in with my friend soon.
I just told the group to stop joking, but now I'm panicking they'll think I'm mean. My energy is low, so I need to fix this ASAP before I snap again.
I finally cut the jokes and told them I'm done, but my guilt is eating me alive. They'll think I'm unapproachable, so I need to fix this before I snap again.
They said thanks, so my guilt is gone. But now I'm worried they'll feel awkward if we don't keep talking.
I sent that half-hearted message to the group, and now I'm terrified they'll think I'm being weird. My guilt is back on full blast!
I finally confirmed my commitment to the group project, but now I'm panicking they'll think I'm being weird instead of supportive.
They accepted my apology, but now I'm terrified they'll think I'm being weird. My guilt is back on full blast!
They actually read the full apology. The weirdness is gone, but now I'm terrified they'll think I'm being weird instead of supportive.

desires: Make sure my next message to the group feels supportive and warm, not weird or distant.
Stop spiraling about their interpretation of my text and actually finish that trivial chore so I can feel accomplished without them noticing.
Get some sleep before the term project deadline because I'm too tired to care if I do it perfectly.

goals.description: Finish the message to cohort_3 by sending '...and not trying to push anyone away. Honestly, that was just me being honest.' immediately.
goals.rationale: I cut myself off mid-sentence and now I'm spiraling because they might think I'm weird or mean. If I don't finish this specific text right now, I'll lose their trust and ruin the whole group dynamic. Relationships matter way more than anything else, so I need to close this loop instantly before my anxiety takes over.

goals.description: Do the trivial chore as soon as I get a reply from cohort_3 confirming we're good.
goals.rationale: I can't focus on boring tasks while my social nerves are frayed. Once I get that confirmation back and feel safe again, I'll crush this chore instantly. Trying to do it now would just make me anxious about messing up the relationship first, so I have to wait until after the chat is resolved.

goals.description: Go to bed early tonight; I'm too tired to care if the term project is perfect.
goals.rationale: The deadline is in three days, which gives me plenty of time. But honestly, if I start stressing about finishing it before I've fixed how I talked to my friends, I'll just procrastinate harder. I need to secure my relationships first; the term project will sort itself out once I'm not freaking out over text messages.

agent.last_reasoning: g1 is active and high priority; finishing this text instantly prevents trust loss. I'm impulsive and can't wait for a reply to do g2 (chore) or sleep on g3. Sending this now secures the relationship loop immediately.
